title: Green Plains Achieves a Milestone as CO2 from Nebraska is Sequestered in Wyoming
content: Green Plains Inc. announced that biogenic carbon dioxide from all three of its Nebraska facilities in Central City, Wood River, and York is now being captured, transported through the Trailblazer pipeline, and permanently sequestered at Tallgrassâ€™ southeast Wyoming sequestration hub. Tallgrass leadership stated that this milestone proves large scale commercial carbon capture and storage is now a reality. Green Plains also received its first 45Z clean fuel production credit payment of about $14 million and expects further payments in early 2026. Year to date, the company has recorded around $26.5 million in 45Z value even before activating carbon capture systems, and credit value per gallon is expected to increase as carbon intensity decreases. Green Plains leadership highlighted that these achievements show strong execution of its low carbon strategy, unlocking new value and strengthening its long term low carbon platform. With carbon capture fully established in Nebraska, the company is positioning itself for stronger performance and sustained growth.
author: Analysts Account
